macro_events.event_summary: Ongoing Iran war has already saddled global companies with $25B+ in direct costs (insurance, shipping, supply disruption, write-downs), with the bill still rising.
primary_drivers: Middle East oil supply risk premium / Strait of Hormuz disruption
Surging maritime war-risk insurance and shipping reroute costs
Defense spending acceleration on sustained conflict

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated with ~2.4M boe/d upstream production largely outside the Middle East; benefits directly from elevated Brent risk premium without proportional asset risk. Strait of Hormuz disruption widens light-sweet differentials in its favor.

Similar Brent-leveraged upstream exposure; Tengiz/Permian production benefits from oil price spike. Has Middle East exposure but primarily through Israel (Tamar/Leviathan gas) which sees increased European LNG demand premium.

Direct beneficiary of sustained munitions consumption — THAAD, PAC-3 Patriot interceptors being drawn down by Israel/US CENTCOM in active Iran engagement. Replenishment contracts already flowing; backlog visibility extends.
Patriot/SM-3/SM-6 interceptors are core consumables in this conflict. Raytheon segment seeing accelerated foreign military sales orders from Gulf states upgrading air defense.
Pure-play Permian producer with zero Middle East operational risk — captures full WTI price spike with no offsetting supply chain hit. Highest oil-price beta among US E&Ps.
Cheniere is the dominant US LNG exporter; European/Asian buyers paying premium to lock in non-Middle East gas as Qatar LNG transit through Hormuz becomes risk-priced. TTF/JKM spreads widening.
Global Hawk ISR demand, B-21 program tailwinds, and solid rocket motor (Aerojet) production for interceptors all see direct pull from sustained conflict operations.

losers.rationale: Significant transatlantic and Asia routes rerouting around Middle East airspace, adding fuel burn and crew costs. Jet fuel crack spreads widening on top of higher Brent. Limited hedging coverage in current cycle.

Heaviest international exposure among US carriers including India/Asia routes that traverse Iranian/Gulf airspace; rerouting adds ~1-3 hours flight time and material fuel cost. $25B global bill explicitly cites aviation rerouting.
Global air freight network heavily uses Gulf hub transits; war-risk surcharges and rerouting hit unit economics. Memphis-Dubai-Asia lane disruption raises cost per package.
Mediterranean and Middle East cruise itineraries cancelled/rerouted; consumer travel demand to region collapses. Carnival has highest Med exposure among cruise operators.
Imported goods cost inflation from shipping reroutes + higher diesel/fuel passes into landed cost. Limited ability to raise prices on price-sensitive consumer; margin compression risk.

Schlumberger has meaningful Middle East services revenue (~25-30%); active conflict disrupts onshore operations, delays projects, and raises personnel evacuation/security costs. Already flagged regional headwinds.
Similar Middle East services exposure though smaller than SLB; operational disruption to Gulf-region completions and pressure pumping contracts.

headlines: Iran war saddles global companies with $25 billion bill - and counting

macro_events.event_summary: China's economy is slowing meaningfully, reigniting expectations of fresh fiscal and monetary stimulus from Beijing.
primary_drivers: Weaker Chinese end-demand pressures companies with high China revenue exposure
Anticipation of PBOC/fiscal stimulus boosts commodity and China-proxy names on expected demand rebound
Lower Chinese growth weighs on industrial commodity prices near-term but stimulus hopes create a bid

beneficiaries.rationale: Domestic Chinese consumption play; benefits disproportionately from consumer-targeted stimulus given low-income customer base most sensitive to subsidies.

losers.rationale: Las Vegas Sands derives ~65% of EBITDA from Macau; Chinese consumer slowdown directly hits gaming revenue and mass-market visitation before any stimulus impact lands.
Wynn Macau is ~70% of property EBITDA; same Macau gross gaming revenue exposure as LVS to weakening mainland consumer.
Freeport copper revenue heavily tied to Chinese construction/property demand; slowdown pressures copper prices near-term despite stimulus-hope offset.
Caterpillar has meaningful China construction equipment exposure; weaker fixed-asset investment from property/infrastructure slowdown hits Asia-Pacific segment.
China is ~22% of Tesla deliveries via Shanghai Gigafactory; consumer slowdown plus intensifying BYD competition pressures both volume and pricing.
Estée Lauder derives ~28% of sales from Asia/travel retail centered on Chinese consumer; prestige beauty is highly discretionary and first to be cut in slowdown.
Greater China is ~15% of Nike revenue and has been a stated weak spot; consumer slowdown extends the China overhang already pressuring the stock.
MGM China Macau exposure (~25% of property EBITDA) makes it the third casino name directly hit by mainland consumer weakness.

headlines: China’s Economy Succumbs to Slowdown and Reignites Stimulus Talk

macro_events.event_summary: Ryanair's CFO warns of a jet fuel supply crunch in Europe that could push weaker European carriers out of business, with Ryanair preparing for an "armageddon" scenario.
primary_drivers: European jet fuel supply tightness raising crack spreads and refining margins
Capacity exit by weaker European carriers benefits surviving low-cost incumbents
Higher fuel cost passthrough pressures unhedged airlines' margins

beneficiaries.rationale: US refiner with high distillate yield; tight European jet fuel market widens transatlantic jet/distillate crack spreads, lifting margins on exports from East Coast/Gulf refineries.
Largest US independent refiner with significant export capacity to Europe and Latin America; benefits directly from elevated jet fuel cracks driven by European supply shortfall.
Danaos and tanker peers aside, product tanker demand rises on transatlantic jet fuel arbitrage; ton-mile demand expansion for clean product carriers shipping jet to Europe.
Scorpio Tankers — pure-play product tanker operator; European jet fuel crunch increases long-haul clean product shipments (US Gulf, Middle East, India to Europe), tightening MR/LR rates.

losers.rationale: JetBlue has weaker balance sheet and minimal fuel hedging; while US-focused, sympathy selling on any global jet fuel cost spike hits the most fuel-cost-sensitive US carrier hardest.
Alaska Air — limited fuel hedging program and lower margin buffer than legacy carriers; rising jet crack spreads globally compress unit economics.
American Airlines does not hedge fuel and carries high leverage; any sustained move higher in global jet fuel pricing (European crunch tightens global balances) directly hits EPS.
Southwest unwound much of its famous hedging program; higher global jet fuel from European tightness pressures CASM-ex with limited offset.

headlines: Ryanair has plans for 'armageddon' scenario as CFO warns weaker European carriers may not survive jet fuel crunch

macro_events.event_summary: US Treasury yields surge alongside a global bond selloff driven by renewed inflation fears.
primary_drivers: Higher long-end yields raise discount rates on long-duration cash flows
Steeper curve / higher rates expand net interest margins for banks
Inflation fears boost demand for real-asset and commodity hedges

beneficiaries.rationale: Charles Schwab earnings are highly leveraged to short-end rates via client cash sweep spreads; rising yields directly expand net interest revenue and ease bond-portfolio AOCI drag.
Large-cap money-center bank with significant asset sensitivity — higher yields lift NIM on floating-rate loan book, and steeper curve supports trading desk activity in rates.
Life insurer with long-dated liability book; higher long yields improve reinvestment income on the float and lift book value of new fixed-income purchases. Direct duration beneficiary.
P&C insurer float is reinvested at higher yields, boosting investment income; also benefits from inflation-driven premium repricing.
Bond rout drives record volumes in interest-rate futures and options (Treasury, SOFR complex), which is CME's largest revenue segment.

losers.rationale: Largest US homebuilder — higher 10Y yields push 30Y mortgage rates up, suppressing affordability and order volumes. Direct rate-sensitive demand channel.
Zillow revenue tied to housing transaction volumes; mortgage rate spike from yield surge directly suppresses listings and Premier Agent demand.
Realty Income is a bond-proxy net-lease REIT with long-duration lease cash flows; rising yields compress cap-rate spreads and pressure the equity.
NextEra carries heavy capex and leverage tied to renewables project finance; higher yields raise cost of capital and compress IRRs on new projects, plus utility bond-proxy de-rating.
High-multiple, long-duration software name trading on 2028+ FCF; among the most sensitive megacap-adjacent names to discount-rate shocks.
Highly levered balance sheet with junk-rated debt; bond rout widens HY spreads and raises refi risk on already-stressed capital structure.

headlines: Treasury yields rise as inflation fears drive global bond rout

macro_events.event_summary: A potential strike by 47,000 Samsung workers in South Korea looms as the country's president pressures for a labor deal, threatening disruption to memory chip and smartphone production.
primary_drivers: Supply disruption risk to global DRAM/NAND memory production
Competitive share gain for rival memory and foundry makers
Potential spot memory price spike benefiting non-Samsung memory suppliers

beneficiaries.rationale: Micron is the most direct US-listed beneficiary — a Samsung production halt would tighten global DRAM/NAND supply, lifting spot memory prices where Micron has near-pure-play exposure (~75%+ of revenue from memory).
Western Digital's NAND flash business (Kioxia JV) gains pricing power and potential share if Samsung NAND output is disrupted.
TSMC is Samsung Foundry's main rival; a prolonged strike would push leading-edge foundry customers (Qualcomm, Nvidia AI chips) further toward TSMC, accelerating share gains.

Apple competes directly with Samsung smartphones globally; disruption to Galaxy production during a key sales window could marginally lift iPhone share, though Apple also sources OLED panels from Samsung Display (offsetting risk).
LG Display would gain OLED panel orders if Samsung Display operations are disrupted by the broader labor action.

losers.rationale: Applied Materials counts Samsung among its top customers for wafer fab equipment; a labor dispute risks capex deferral and slows tool pull-ins for memory fabs.
Lam Research has heavy memory-equipment exposure with Samsung as a key customer; production stoppage threatens near-term shipments and acceptance revenue.
KLA's process control tools sell into Samsung's memory and foundry lines; strike-driven capex hesitation hits backlog conversion.
Samsung is a major EUV customer; extended labor disruption could delay lithography tool deliveries and acceptance milestones.
Broadcom supplies networking/custom silicon partly fabbed at Samsung Foundry; any output disruption pressures delivery commitments for AI accelerator customers.
Dell PC and server builds depend on Samsung DRAM/NAND; a memory price spike from supply disruption compresses hardware gross margins.
HP PC margins are sensitive to DRAM/NAND spot prices; Samsung strike-driven memory inflation is a direct COGS headwind.

headlines: Samsung strike involving 47,000 workers looms as South Korea’s president urges labor deal
